name: zx (Google's Shell Scripting for Node.js)

auth.notes: Local shell scripting tool; no auth

performance.notes: Shell process spawning overhead; negligible for scripting use cases

agent_readiness.notes: Tagged template literal for shell commands: `const result = await $'ls -la'`. Auto-escapes arguments. Built-in: chalk, fetch, glob, yaml, minimist, os, path. ProcessOutput with stdout/stderr/exitCode. Pipe support: await $'cmd'.pipe($'next'). stdin/stdout. retry() for resilient scripts. spinner() progress. Quiet mode. within() for scoped environment. TypeScript-native. .mjs scripts or zx CLI. Bun supports zx. Better than execa for shell-heavy scripts; execa better for programmatic subprocess control.

gotchas: Shell injection risk if not using template literals (use $'cmd' not $.run())
Slightly slower than raw child_process for simple cases
best_when: Replacing Bash scripts with typed JavaScript/TypeScript
CI/CD automation scripts needing complex logic
Development tooling scripts mixing shell commands and JS logic
Teams preferring JS over Bash for maintainability
avoid_when: Simple one-liner shell scripts (raw execa or child_process simpler)
summary: zx is Google's tool for writing shell scripts in JavaScript using tagged template literals: `await $'git status'`. Auto-escapes arguments, streams output, and includes chalk, fetch, glob, yaml built-in. TypeScript-native. Replaces Bash scripts with maintainable JS logic. Best for DevOps automation and CI scripts where complex logic and shell commands mix. More ergonomic than raw child_process or execa for shell-heavy work.
